title: What is Next.js?
summary: Next.js is a framework built on top of React.
It adds routing, rendering choices, and a backend.
It handles many setup decisions for you.
It is the most popular React framework.

sections.text: React gives you the pieces to build a user interface, but a real website needs more, pages, fast first loads, a way to talk to a server. Next.js is a framework that wraps React and provides all of that, so you build less plumbing yourself.

sections.title: What Next.js Adds
sections.text: React on its own is just the view layer. Next.js builds on top of it and adds the things a full website needs, routing between pages, choices about how pages are rendered, and even a place to put backend code. You still write React, but with a lot of decisions already made for you.

sections.title: Why a Framework
sections.text: With plain React you assemble your own tools for routing, building, and data. A framework like Next.js bundles sensible choices together, so you spend less time wiring things up and more time building. The trade off is you follow its conventions, which is usually a fair deal.

media.alt: React at the center with Next.js adding routing, rendering, and backend around it

sections.title: A Kit Home vs Raw Materials
sections.text: Plain React is like a pile of quality building materials. You can build anything, but you decide every detail. Next.js is like a kit home. The big decisions are made, the parts fit together, and you focus on making it yours. You give up some freedom for a lot of speed.

media.alt: Raw building materials beside a ready to assemble kit home

sections.title: Where Next.js Fits
items: You already know React for building the interface.
Next.js wraps React and adds the missing pieces.
It gives you routing between pages.
It lets you choose how each page is rendered.
It even provides a place for backend code.

sections.text: Next.js is made by a company called Vercel, which also offers easy hosting for it. That tight fit between the framework and its hosting is part of why it became so popular.

terms.definition: A framework built on top of React.
A toolkit that bundles many decisions for you.
The view layer Next.js builds on.
Moving between the pages of a site.
An agreed way of doing things the framework expects.
